case_title: ERICK FALLON VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL

docket_entries.description: REPLY IN SUPP OF MTN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQ FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCS SET ONE (TRANSACTION ID # 15152015) FILED BY PLAINTIFF FALLON, ERICK
ORDER ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUEMTNS, SET ONE, AND FOR PROSPECTIVE MONETARY SANCTIONS
STIPULATION AND ORDER FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-21-2015 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF ERICK FALLON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S RREQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. THE COURT AGREES WITH THE DEFENDANT CERTAIN DOCUMENT REQUEST CATEGORIES WHICH SEEK "ANY AND ALL" DOCUMENTS IN A SONG-BEVERLY CONSUMER WARRANTY ACT ACTION IS OVERBROAD AND POTENTIALLY COVERS MILLIONS OF DOCUMENTS. THE COURT INTENDS TO MODIFY, WHEN NEEDED, EACH SPECIFIC DOCUMENT REQUEST CATEGORY TO FOCUS ON ALLEGATIONS OF THE SUBJECT VEHICLE'S ALLEGED TRANSMISSION PROBLEM. THE COURT FINDS DONLEN V. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, 217 CAL. APP. 4TH 138 (2013) AND DOPPES V. BENTLEY MOTORS, INC., 174 CAL. APP 4TH 967 (2009) SUPPORT THE BROAD SCOPE OF DISCOVERABLE DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE REQUESTED. THE COURT AGREES WITH DEFENDANT A PROTECTIVE ORDER/CONFIDENTIALITY ORDER IS APPROPRIATE FOR MANY OF THE DOCUMENTS TO BE PRODUCED. PROPOSED ORDER SHALL SUBMITTED ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 1, 2015. DEFENDANT SHALL BE PERMITTED TO PROVIDE A SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION ON PROTECTIVE ORDER ISSUES. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, REPORTER: KATHRYN LLOYD, CSR# 5955, TEL NO. 925-548-9114 (302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2016 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2015 Continued To May-16-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant'S Motion For A Protective Order In Connection With Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Sep-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. BENJAMIN BECK OF THE STRATEGIC LEGAL PRACTICE, TEL. NO. 1-310-277-1040 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NEIL GIELEGHEM, TEL. NO. 1-310-284-3252 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. H. PAUL EFSTRATIS & ATTY. JAMIE M. CHENG OF THE LECLAIR/RYAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO.M 1-415-913-4957 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
KATHRYN LLOYD, TEL. NO. 1-925-548-9114, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendant's Motion For A Protective Order In Connection With Production Of Documents is granted. Parties to meet and confer regarding proposed order. Judge Pro tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Kathryn Lloyd, CSR # 5955 =302/jpt

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltffs Rrequest For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NEIL GIELEGHEM, ESQ. of the Law Offices of Neil Gieleghem, Tel No. 310-284-3252 and atty. BENJEMAN BECK, ESQ. of Strategic Legal Practices APC, Tel No. 310-277-1040 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. PAUL EFSTRATIS, ESQ. and RYAN J. GREENSPAN, ESQ. of LeClairRyan LLP, Tel No. 415-913-4957 appearing for the Defendant (Mr. Greenspan appearing telephonically).

RULING - Matter argued by counsel. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF ERICK FALLON'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Rrequest For Production Of Documents, Set One, the motion to compel is granted. The Court agrees with the defendant certain document request categories which seek "any and all" documents in a Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act action is overbroad and potentially covers millions of documents. The Court intends to modify, when needed, each specific document request category to focus on allegations of the subject vehicle's alleged transmission problem. The Court finds Donlen v. Ford Motor Company, 217 Cal. App. 4th 138 (2013) and Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc., 174 Cal. App 4th 967 (2009) support the broad scope of discoverable documentary evidence requested. The Court agrees with defendant a Protective Order/Confidentiality Order is appropriate for many of the documents to be produced. Proposed order shall submitted on or before September 1, 2015. Defendant shall be permitted to provide a supplemental declaration on protective order issues. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: Sean Kane, Reporter: Kathryn Lloyd, CSR# 5955, Tel No. 925-548-9114 (302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltffs Rrequest For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Jul-21-2015 Continued To Aug-20-15 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NEIL GIELEGHEM, ESQ. of Gieleghem Law Office, Tel No. 310-284-3252, and atty. BENJEMEN BECK, ESQ. of Strategic Legal Practices APC, Tel No. 310-277-1040 appearing for the plaintiff (Mr. Gieleghem appearing telephonically);
Atty. PAUL EFSTRATIS of LeClair Ryan LLP, Tel No. 415-913-4957 appearing for Defendant Ford Motor Company.

The Court's tentative ruling required parties to appear at hearing. Parties stipulate to matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Argument presented by counsel. PLAINTIFF ERICK FALLON'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One is continued to August 20, 2015 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 302, Discovery Calendar. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Rhiannon Smith, CSR #13265, Tel No. 619-318-1225. (302/JPT)
